Table rules: Remove unused using directives (Aspose.Words.Drawing, Aspose.Words.Tables) that are not needed for this task.
Range rules: Use the Document.Range.Fields collection directly – it already implements IEnumerable<Field>.
Do not use NodeType.Field – it does not exist; use doc.Range.Fields to enumerate fields.
Document merge/insert rules: Add the missing namespace import: `using Aspose.Words.Fields;` to expose `Field` and `FieldType`.
Remove the non‑existent `Aspose.Words.Import` namespace; `NodeImporter` lives directly under `Aspose.Words`.
Remove the non‑existent namespace import `using Aspose.Words.Import;` – NodeImporter lives directly in `Aspose.Words`.
Import nodes with NodeImporter to preserve source formatting.
Use NodeImporter with ImportFormatMode.KeepSourceFormatting to preserve formatting.
Loading rules: Do not use LoadOptions when they are not required; a simple Document constructor with the file path is sufficient.
Keep the document loading and TOC removal logic unchanged.
Keep existing logic for loading, modifying, and saving the document.
Saving/Export rules: Save the document with SaveFormat.Dotm to produce a macro‑enabled template.
Save the document using SaveFormat.WordML to produce WORDML (XML) output.
Use ImageSaveOptions with SaveFormat.Png to control page rendering; set PageSet to render only the first page.
Include the Aspose.Words.Saving namespace for ImageSaveOptions and PageSet.
PsSaveOptions does not require setting SaveFormat, but setting it explicitly is harmless.
When saving to TIFF, use ImageSaveOptions to control pagination (optional but recommended).
Save the final document using SaveFormat.Epub.
Save the result with `SaveFormat.Dot` to produce a .dot template file.
Specify the save format explicitly (SaveFormat.Doc) when saving as a DOC file.
Save the document with the desired format using SaveFormat enum.
Use the correct class name MarkdownSaveOptions for saving as Markdown.
Keep the original logic: load DOC, remove TOC fields, save as MHTML.
Save the resulting document using SaveFormat.Pdf.
Use `ImageSaveOptions` with `PageSet` to render only the first page when saving to PNG.
Keep the rest of the logic unchanged – load document, remove TOC fields, and save as TIFF using ImageSaveOptions.
Save with SaveFormat.Docx to produce a DOCX file.
Specify the save format explicitly (SaveFormat.Dotm) when saving as a DOTM file.
Save the final document with a `.docm` extension to produce a macro‑enabled Word file.
When saving as a template, the `.dot` extension is sufficient; you may also explicitly pass `SaveFormat.Dot` if desired.
Save the document with the .dotm extension to produce a macro‑enabled template.
Save the final document with SaveFormat.Dotx.
Save the document using SaveFormat.Dot to produce a .dot template file.
Save the document with an .rtf extension (doc.Save("OutputDocument.rtf")).
Images/Shapes/OLE rules: Keep the rest of the logic unchanged – removal of nodes and image saving are valid.
Keep the existing image save logic; no changes needed for JPEG output.
Ensure the correct namespaces are imported (Aspose.Words and Aspose.Words.Drawing).
Keep the Shape type check (ShapeType.TextBox) to target only text boxes.
Ensure all required namespaces are included (System, Aspose.Words, Aspose.Words.Drawing).
Keep the required using directives (System, Aspose.Words, Aspose.Words.Drawing).
Include the required namespace Aspose.Words.Drawing for Shape and ShapeType.
Use the correct NodeType.Shape constant to retrieve shape nodes.
Layout rules: NodeType enum contains only Footnote; there is no Endnote value.
Endnotes are represented by Footnote nodes with FootnoteType.Endnote.
Check the Footnote.FootnoteType property to distinguish footnotes from endnotes.
NodeType does not contain an Endnote member; both footnotes and endnotes are represented by NodeType.Footnote.
Remove the separate Endnote removal block and handle endnotes together with footnotes.
Use the Aspose.Words.Footnote class for both footnotes and endnotes (there is no separate Endnote class).
NodeType only contains Footnote; endnotes are identified via Footnote.FootnoteType == FootnoteType.Endnote.
Use NodeType.Footnote for both footnotes and endnotes (there is no NodeType.Endnote).
Use NodeType.Footnote only – Aspose.Words represents both footnotes and endnotes with the Footnote node type; there is no NodeType.Endnote.
Use NodeType.Footnote for both footnotes and endnotes; there is no NodeType.Endnote.
Iterate over the collection of Footnote nodes and call Remove() on each.
Import Aspose.Words.Notes to access Footnote and FootnoteType if you need to differentiate types.
No separate Endnote handling is required because endnotes are also Footnote nodes.
Add the missing namespace import: `using Aspose.Words.Notes;` to access the `Footnote` class.
Cast each node to `Footnote` before calling `Remove()`.
NodeType does not have an Endnote member – both footnotes and endnotes are retrieved with NodeType.Footnote.
Do not reference a non‑existent 'Endnote' type; use Footnote.FootnoteType to differentiate footnote vs endnote.
Remove the non‑existent `Aspose.Words.Notes` namespace; footnote classes live in `Aspose.Words`.
Footnote and endnote objects are both of type `Footnote`; removing all `NodeType.Footnote` nodes eliminates both.
Footnotes and endnotes are both instances of the Footnote class; use NodeType.Footnote to retrieve them.
Use the Aspose.Words.Notes namespace for Footnote and FootnoteSeparator types.
Do not reference NodeType.Endnote – endnotes are represented by the Footnote class with IsEndnote property, and GetChildNodes(NodeType.Footnote) returns both footnotes and endnotes.
Remove notes by iterating over doc.GetChildNodes(NodeType.Footnote, true) and calling Remove() on each Footnote instance.
Use NodeType.Footnote only – Aspose.Words does not expose a separate NodeType.Endnote.
If you also need to delete footnote separators, uncomment the optional block.
Use the correct NodeType members (Footnote and Endnote) that exist in Aspose.Words.
If required, also remove the footnote/endnote separator symbols via the FootnoteSeparators collection.
Footnote indices are zero‑based; adjust `footnoteIndex` to target the correct footnote.
Use `doc.GetChild(NodeType.Footnote, index, true)` to locate a footnote anywhere in the document.
Call `footnote.Remove()` to delete the footnote node.
Use fully qualified Aspose.Words types (`Document`, `Paragraph`, `Section`, `Node`).
Ensure the destination document has at least one section by calling `EnsureMinimum()`.
Keep the `using Aspose.Words;` directive; it provides access to Document, Paragraph, Section, NodeImporter, etc.
Use Document.EnsureMinimum() to guarantee a section and body exist before appending nodes.
Append the imported paragraph to an existing section/body rather than creating a new Section manually (simpler and avoids duplicate sections).
